items.hook: He was appointed suffect consul some time between 73 and 76.
items.body: Gaius Vettulenus Civica Cerealis was a Roman senator who held at least one office in the service of the emperor. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Seinfeld actor apologises to reality star for 'inappropriate' sketch when she was 17
items.body: Jason Alexander said he "truly regrets" the Funny Or Die sketch he appeared in with Courtney Stodden.

items.hook: Paños are pen or pencil drawings on fabric, a form of prison artwork made in the Southwest United States created primarily by pintos, or Chicanos who are or have been incarcerated.

items.hook: The Greek military junta collapses, and former Prime Minister Konstantinos Karamanlis is invited to lead the new government, beginning Greece's metapolitefsi era.
items.body: The Greek junta or Regime of the Colonels was a right-wing military junta that ruled Greece from 1967 to 1974. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Carnegie Library in Guthrie, Oklahoma, is a building at 406 East Oklahoma Avenue.
items.body: Constructed in 1901, It was the second Carnegie-funded library built in Oklahoma and the oldest one still in existence. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Aleksander Jełowicki was a Polish writer, poet, translator and publisher.
items.body: He was a veteran of the November Uprising, deputy to the Sejm of Congress Poland for the Haisyn powiat and political exile in France, where he was a social activist, superior of the Polish Catholic Mission in Paris and monk. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit

items.hook: Fantastic Voyage is a 1966 American science fiction adventure film directed by Richard Fleischer and written by Harry Kleiner, based on a story by Otto Klement and Jerome Bixby.
items.body: The film is about a submarine crew which is shrunk to microscopic size and ventures into the body of injured scientist Dr. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The Odyssey film fans inspired to go back to the source
items.body: Book sales and audiobook figures for Homer's original poem rise sharply after the film's release.

items.hook: A wildfire in East Attica kills at least 102 people. It is the deadliest wildfire in the history of Greece.
items.body: A series of wildfires in Greece, during the 2018 European heat wave, began in the coastal areas of Attica in July 2018. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Medstead is a village and civil parish in the East Hampshire district of Hampshire, England.
items.body: Its nearest town is Alton, which lies 4.3 miles (6.9 km) northeast of the village. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The only successful hijacking of an El Al aircraft takes place when a Boeing 707 carrying ten crew and 38 passengers is taken over by three members of the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine. The aircraft was en route from Rome, to Lod, Israel.
items.body: El Al Israel Airlines Ltd., trading as El Al is the flag carrier of the State of Israel. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Watch the total solar eclipse with ESA in León
items.body: Discover the science behind one of nature's most extraordinary phenomena and experience a total solar eclipse with the European Space Agency (ESA), the City of León and the University of León.

items.hook: BFI reveals next director of Filmmaking Funds
items.body: New director will take over from Mia Bays in October.

items.hook: While the Mora Rebellion continues, Greeks capture Monemvasia Castle. Turkish troops and citizens are transferred to Asia Minor's coasts.
items.body: The Greek War of Independence, also known as the Greek Revolution or the Greek Revolution of 1821, was a successful war of independence fought by Greek revolutionaries against the Ottoman Empire from 1821 to 1829. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: In Catalonia, Spain, the Unified Socialist Party of Catalonia is founded through the merger of Socialist and Communist parties.
items.body: Catalonia is an autonomous community of Spain, designated as a nationality by its Statute of Autonomy. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The International Agreement on the Neutrality of Laos is signed.
items.body: The International Agreement on the Neutrality of Laos was an international agreement signed in Geneva on July 23, 1962, between 14 states, including Laos, as a result of the International Conference on the Settlement of the Laotian Question, which lasted from May 16, 1961, to July 23, 1962. One of those things you can't un-know.
